exemplar.title: To what extent has the immigration of refugees and asylum seekers from the Middle East to Germany since 2015 impacted the rates of violent crime?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has the immigration of refugees and asylum seekers from the Middle East to Germany since 2015 impacted the rates of violent crime?

strengths: Clear and appropriately narrow research question, maintained focus throughout.
Adequate communication of topic scope with signposting in title, abstract, and introduction.
Some evidence of informed methodology selection (e.g., justification of BKA data).
weaknesses: Opening sections contain vague, repetitive statements that dilute precision.
Methodology lacks explicit explanation of sourceâselection criteria and dataâhandling procedures.
Some factual tangents (e.g., Sweden, Poland) are only loosely tied back to the central focus.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work aligns with the 3-4 band: the research question is clearly stated and narrow, and communication is adequate. However, the introduction is uneven, and the methodology, while relevant, lacks full transparency. A midâband score of 3 reflects these strengths and limitations.

strengths: Regular insertion of quotations, graphs, and statistics that match the narrative points.
Reasonable grasp of key concepts: migration policy, crime definitions, demographic data.
Consistent use of basic terminology (âasylum seekerâ, ârefugeeâ, âviolent crimeâ).
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on sensational news media with little critique of bias.
Incomplete citations and missing source details for images and some data.
Academic concepts (e.g., securitisation, structural violence) are largely absent, and occasional colloquial language appears.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Knowledge and Understanding falls in the middle band (3-4): application of sources is mostly relevant but not consistently rigorous; topic knowledge is clear but sometimes anecdotal; terminology use is largely accurate but not fully sophisticated. A score of 3 captures this balanced appraisal.

strengths: Uses a breadth of data (national statistics, academic studies) generally relevant to the research question.
Develops logical analytical threads linking pre- and post-2015 crime trends.
Identifies key insights (e.g., disproportionate suspect share) and attempts to question over-reporting.
weaknesses: Analysis often stops at correlation without controlling for confounding variables.
Overreliance on emotive anecdotes rather than systematic evidence.
Discussion lacks sustained critical depth, especially on alternative explanations and limitations.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Critical Thinking aligns with the bottom of the 4-6 band: research quality and analysis are more than minimal but not yet consistently âgoodâ, and evaluation is adequate but shallow. A score of 4 reflects these adequate yet under-developed critical thinking skills.

strengths: Conventional Extended Essay structure with table of contents, headings, and sub-headings.
Tables, graphs, and quotations are generally clear and legible.
Title page, word count, and bibliography are present.
weaknesses: Table of contents page references are misaligned, disrupting navigation.
Figure captions and footnote formatting are inconsistent or unclear.
Minor layout issues (spacing, inline math formatting errors) impede smooth readability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation shows some correct layout and structure but is weakened by misaligned references, missing captions, and formatting inconsistencies. These shortcomings place it in the 1-2 band; a score of 2 accurately represents the overall presentation quality.

strandSelections.reasoning: There is a logical threadâcrime trends before and after 2015, proportion of suspects vs populationâbut analysis often stops at correlation, with limited control for confounding variables. Some sections rely on emotive cases rather than systematic evidence. Conclusions are present but only partially supported, fitting the lower 4-6 range.

The student gathers an adequate breadth of data (national statistics, academic study, news pieces) that are generally relevant to the RQ, though reliability varies and alternative explanations are not systematically sourced. This reflects the lower end of the 4-6 band: research is more than minimal but not yet âgoodâ throughout.

The paper follows a conventional EE structure: abstract, introduction, analytical chapters, conclusion, bibliography. Headings, sub-headings and table of contents aid navigation, although some sections (e.g. broader perspective) interrupt flow and page references in ToC are misaligned. Overall structure supports understanding, matching the 3-4 band.

The essay demonstrates a reasonable grasp of migration policy, crime definitions and demographic data, yet explanations of causality (e.g. testosterone, culture clash) are simplistic and occasionally speculative. Knowledge is organised but sometimes anecdotal. This aligns with clear but only partially effective use of sourcesâmid-band.

Arguments are developed (e.g., socio-economic factors, age-gender structure) and the student occasionally questions over-reporting, yet evaluation of counter-narratives (e.g., studies finding no link) is shallow. The discussion stays pertinent but lacks sustained critical depth. This represents adequate rather than âgoodâ critical evaluationâbottom of the 4-6 band.

Tables, graphs and quotations are formatted clearly, the title page, word count and bibliography are present, and visual aids are legible. Minor issuesâmissing figure captions, inconsistent spacingâexist but do not impede readability. Layout considerations are therefore present and largely correct, corresponding to the 3-4 band.

Terms such as âasylum seekerâ, ârefugeeâ, âviolent crimeâ and âintegrationâ are defined early and used fairly consistently. Nonetheless, academic political-science concepts (securitisation, structural violence, social disorganisation) are largely absent and some casual language (âwent down a different pathâ) appears. Usage is mostly accurate but not fully sophisticated, fitting the 3-4 band.

The research question, scope (2015-present, violent crime, ME refugees) and key concepts are signposted in the title, abstract and introduction, so the reader understands what will be explored. Nonetheless the opening sections contain vague, repetitive statements (e.g. âimmigration can be beneficialâ¦ but also negativeâ) and some factual tangents that cloud precision. Explanation of the temporal and conceptual boundaries is present but uneven; for example, the student does not specify which crime indicators will be used until later. Hence communication is adequate rather than comprehensive, aligning with the 3-4 band.

The student works almost entirely with secondary literature, press reports and official statistics; no primary fieldwork or systematic comparative method is outlined. Source choice is relevant (BKA, Statista, academic paper by Lange & Sommerfeld) but the selection criteria, reliability checks and data-handling procedures are not explained. Thus the methodology is mostly complete but shows only some evidence of informed selection, meriting mid-band marks.

The question is clearly stated, appropriately narrow (single host country, single migrant group, defined time-frame) and remains visible throughout the essay. However, some paragraphs drift into Swedish and Polish examples that are only loosely tied back to the focus. This indicates partial but not complete sharpness. Overall this fits the top of the 3-4 band.

Quotations, graphs and statistics are inserted regularly and normally match the point being made; however, several sensational anecdotes from Fox News, Daily Mail or European Conservative are used with little scrutiny. Citations are sometimes incomplete and images lack full source details. Application is therefore mostly relevant but not consistently rigorous, corresponding to the middle band.

annotations.comment: The introduction to the German evidence section is clearly signposted and supports reader navigation.
The research question is clearly stated and appropriately narrow, maintaining focus throughout the essay.
The topic statement is too generic; refine it to reflect the specific scope (Middle Eastern refugees in Germany post-2015) rather than âcrime and asylum seekersâ broadly.
Page references in the table of contents are misaligned, which disrupts navigation and may confuse the reader.
The structure of sub-headings in the contents is over-complex; simplify to improve clarity and flow.
The opening sentences are overly general and introduce vague claims; target Germany post-2015 directly to sharpen the focus.
The introduction effectively previews the scopeâMiddle Eastern refugees in Germany and violent crimeâproviding clear conceptual boundaries.
The list of crime causation factors contains an OCR artefact â\(\mathbb{Z}^{\mathbb{Z}}\)â which should be removed.
Applying Amnesty International definitions strengthens conceptual clarity around refugees, asylum seekers, and migrants.
Sections on Poland and Sweden drift from the central focus on Germany, making the essayâs sharpness of focus inconsistent.
Citation of Wikipedia for gun-crime data is insufficiently scholarly; replace with peer-reviewed or official EU criminology reports.
Include a comparative baseline when discussing Lower Saxony data to contextualize the 10.4% increase.
Phrasing like âwent down a different pathâ is colloquial and undercuts academic tone; revise for precision.
Clear explanation of why BKA data is reliable demonstrates informed methodology selection.
The figure caption and footnote formatting are unclearâuse consistent numbering, captions, and source details.
Recognition that refugees form a smaller population share yet higher suspect share provides strong analytical insight.
Heavy reliance on The European Conservative without critique of bias weakens the reliability of secondary evidence.
Individual case anecdotes lack scrutiny; broader statistical analysis should be prioritized.
The New Yearâs Eve 2015/16 discussion relies on a single source; incorporate additional studies for balance.
The juvenileâcrime analysis insightfully links developmental vulnerability to manipulation by criminal groups.
Discussion of Lange & Sommerfeld study is well chosen; it integrates an academic source and acknowledges reporting bias.
The discussion on culture and religion is truncated mid-sentence and lacks appropriate citation completion; finish and cite fully.
No discussion of confounding variables (e.g., reporting rates) is present when interpreting crime trends.
Socio-economic factors are described but methodology for selecting these factors is not explained; clarify your selection criteria.
The inline math â\(242.207\)â is formatted incorrectly and likely represents a typo; correct numerical presentation.
Cross-referencing Statista and DW data to confirm youth crime trends strengthens analytic validity.
Conclusion restates findings but omits critical reflection on limitations and alternative explanations.
Excessive reliance on news media sources at the expense of peer-reviewed research undermines academic rigor.
Word count is provided and within the 4,000-word limit, meeting the formatting requirement.
Streamline the works cited list by ensuring each reference follows the same formatting rules (e.g., punctuation, italics).
Bibliography entries are inconsistently formatted and not alphabetically ordered; apply a single citation style throughout.
